want: the framework to search GitHub for matching issues when I provide free-text input to /isdlc add
so_that: my backlog items are automatically linked to existing GitHub issues without me having to look up the issue number

want: to see a list of matching GitHub issues and select the correct one
so_that: I can confirm the right issue is linked to my backlog item

want: the backlog item to be linked to the selected GitHub issue with proper source metadata
so_that: the item is traceable from the local backlog to GitHub for team-level visibility

want: the option to create a new GitHub issue when no existing match is found
so_that: I don't have to leave the iSDLC workflow to create an issue manually

want: to be able to skip GitHub issue linking and proceed with a manual entry
so_that: I am not forced to use GitHub for every backlog item (e.g., quick prototypes, personal tasks)

want: the add flow to work normally even when the gh CLI is not installed or authenticated
so_that: the framework never blocks my workflow due to missing external tooling

want: the /isdlc analyze command to use the same reverse-lookup when it triggers an add for unmatched free-text input
so_that: the linking behavior is consistent regardless of whether I use add or analyze
